## 

# Design Prologue {#design-prologue}

Meshery uses Nighthawk as one of its load generators to run performance benchmarks. As Nighthawk supports [adaptive load control](https://github.com/envoyproxy/nighthawk/tree/main/api/adaptive_load) as a feature, Meshery can leverage this to build a custom load controller to run performance benchmarks.

As Meshery uses Nighthawk to manage the lifecycle of Nighthawk, this additional functionality needs to be included into Nighthawk to be used by Meshery.

By default the adaptive load controller in Nighthawk runs benchmarks with different RPS values and based on the latency, it adjusts the RPS value. But, with its custom plugin ability, we can bring in our own inputs as well as metrics to measure.

With the metrics from these tests, Meshery should also adjust the resiliency characteristics of the mesh automatically so as to improve these metrics and in turn improve performance.

| Resources [GetNighthawk Project Description](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1lHfMo4iIx2WXFZIspfHyxTsPR1T63_2IV5NUkgxoo0w/edit) [GetNighthawk Repo](https://github.com/layer5io/getnighthawk) [Adaptive Load Controller API (Nighthawk)](https://github.com/envoyproxy/nighthawk/tree/main/api/adaptive_load) [Adaptive Controller](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1uQBrocLLqudO-xKSVL38d8w9BXlNOLeB/edit#slide=id.p1) |
| :---- |

# Design Goals {#design-goals}

The designs in this specification should result in enabling:

1. **Using adaptive load control feature of Nighthawk**  
   1. Meshery should be able to additionally use the adaptive load controller capabilities from Nighthawk

2. **Adding custom plugins for metrics and values**  
   1. Define custom plugins for capturing metrics and changing values for running performance benchmarks  
        
3. **Define MeshMark**  
   1. Define MeshMark, a performance index/scale to provide people the ability to weigh the value vs overhead of their service mesh
